# Kiara Capital — Insights

> Venture capital intelligence, startup ecosystem analysis, financial innovation and AI — in Brazil and across the globe.

## About

Kiara Capital is a venture capital firm backing exceptional founders building at the intersection of AI and financial services in Brazil and Latin America. We focus on the application and data layer — not infrastructure — where we believe the most defensible fintech companies will be built.

**Website:** https://www.kiara.capital/
**Insights blog:** https://insights.kiara.capital/

## Investment Thesis

- AI at the application and data layer — not infrastructure, not LLMs
- Agentic finance — credit, fraud, compliance, wealth management rebuilt from scratch
- Data moats — proprietary data as defensible competitive advantage
- Intelligent workflows — AI transforming SMB ERPs, accounting, fiduciary services, KYC
- Accessible finance — making complex financial processes scalable
- Brazil and Latin America as structural opportunity

## Portfolio

Astride, Payana, Nexa Finance, Rinne, Lugui, BOND, W (stealth)

## Articles

Kiara Insights publishes analysis on venture capital, artificial intelligence, financial innovation, startups, and regulation — written in the analytical, contrarian voice of Kiara's investment team. Content is for informational purposes only and does not constitute investment advice.

### Artificial Intelligence

- [OpenAI Used Its Own AI to Build a Chip. The Nine-Month Timeline Is the Most Important Number in the Announcement.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/ai-openai-jalapeno-chip/): OpenAI designed Jalapeño — a custom LLM inference ASIC — in nine months with Broadcom, using its own models to accelerate chip design. Standard development takes 2-3 years. The compression changes inference cost curves and who can build AI products at scale.

### Venture Capital

- [68% Markdown in Three Years. The Pre-ChatGPT Unicorns Are Learning What Moats Are Actually Made Of.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/vc-fallen-unicorns-chatgpt-reset/): 220+ former unicorns have fallen below $1B — down 68% for 2021 vintages, 52% for 2022. Enterprise SaaS is the largest fallen category. The VC bifurcation between AI-native and AI-disrupted is structural, not cyclical.
- [June 25 Sent $850 Million to One Idea: AI That Actually Works at Scale](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/vc-execution-layer-june25/): In a single day, VCs wrote eight-figure checks to Runpod, Sail Research, Scaled Cognition, Alan, Arca, and Redo — none of which make a better model. All of them solve execution, reliability, and production-grade deployment at regulated enterprise scale.
- [Inference Is the New Cloud. Baseten's $1.5 Billion Round Is the First Real Proof.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/vc-baseten-inference-1-5b/): Baseten raised $1.5 billion on June 22 at a $13 billion valuation, processing one billion AI inference calls daily. Revenue grew 20x year-over-year — inference is where the durable value in AI settles.
- [Government Is AI's Least Obvious and Most Durable Enterprise Customer. Peregrine's $6.8B Valuation Says So.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/vc-peregrine-government-ai/): Peregrine Technologies raised $250 million at a $6.8 billion valuation to expand AI operations software from state agencies into federal markets. The compliance requirement most AI vendors treat as an obstacle is what Peregrine built into its architecture.

### Financial Innovation

- [88% of AI Capital Lands in the US. The Best Infrastructure for Agentic Finance Is Somewhere Else.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/fi-brazil-agentic-finance/): In Q1 2026, 88% of AI venture funding went to US companies. Brazil and Mexico attracted under $300M in AI startup capital across all of 2025. Yet Brazil operates the world's most active real-time payment network, live Open Finance APIs, and a programmable tokenization layer — exactly what agentic finance requires.
- [The Model Was Never the Product. Taktile's $110M Round Proves the Real AI Moat in Finance Is What Happens After the Prediction.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/fi-taktile-agentic-decisioning-110m/): Taktile raised $110 million from Goldman Sachs, Tiger Global, and Balderton to build the agentic decisioning platform for banks and insurers. One customer hit 95% automation in B2B underwriting. $90M in projected claims savings for one insurer.
- [Brazil's Salary Accounts Are Getting Recurring Payments. The Structural Shift Is Bigger Than It Looks.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/fi-pix-automatico-conta-salario/): Starting July 2026, Banco Central allows Pix Automático to draw from conta-salário accounts — payroll accounts used exclusively by tens of millions of Brazilian workers. The credit card's last remaining grip on subscription payments just weakened further.
- [Brazil's Banco Central Turned Two Financial Steps Into One. That's Not a UX Story.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/fi-pix-openfinance-jornada/): The "optimized journey" merging Open Finance data consent and Pix payment authorization into a single tap builds the consent architecture for agentic finance to operate at scale in Brazil.
- [Brazil's Banco Central Mapped the World's Most Ambitious Financial Stack. The Overlooked Part Is What's Already Running.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/fi-bc-drex-roadmap-2029/): The BC's 2026-2029 plan converges Pix, Open Finance, Drex, and RWA tokenization into a single programmable financial layer. Drex still has no confirmed…
- [Brazil's Open Finance Rails Are Live. The R$42 Billion Opportunity Isn't About Sharing Data â€” It's About Who Writes the Credit Algorithm.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/fi-open-finance-ai-credit-brasil/): Open Finance passes 34 million consents in Brazil with data flowing across credit, investments, and payments at scale. PwC projects R$42 billion in new…
- [Ramp Is a $44 Billion Company Because CFOs Didn't Budget for AI Spending. That's Not a Coincidence.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/fi-ramp-44b/): Ramp raised $750 million at a $44 billion valuation on a single thesis: CFOs didn't plan for AI spending growth and don't have the tools to manage it. The…
- [Credit Cards Will Lose 80% of Subscription Transactions to Pix AutomÃ¡tico. That's Not the Number Worth Tracking.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/fi-pix-automatico-data-layer/): Pix AutomÃ¡tico launched this month, and every analyst is writing about credit card displacement. The story they're missing: 60 million Brazilians who had…
- [Brazil's Pix Anti-Fraud Upgrade Isn't a Security Story. It's a Moat Story.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/fi-pix-med2-moat/): MED 2.0 tracks fraudulent flows through five subsequent accounts, enables self-service recovery in 11 days, and could cut successful fraud by 40%. The Banco…
- [The Founder of Circle Is Building a Bank for AI Agents. The Timing Is Not an Accident.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/fi-catena-labs/): Sean Neville raised $30M Series A to build financial rails for AI agents â€” and filed for a national trust bank charter at the OCC. - [Mercury Isn't a Neobank Anymore. The OCC Just Changed Everything.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/fi-mercury-occ/): Mercury raised $200M at a $5.2B valuation right after OCC conditional approval for a national bank charter. The partner-bank dependency model ends here.…
- [Brazil's RWA Market Grew 2,249% in a Year. The Rest of the World Is Still Debating the Framework.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/fi-rwa-brazil/): Brazil's tokenized real-world asset market went from R$122M to R$2.87B in twelve months â€” not because of crypto enthusiasm, but because the Banco Central…
- [Drex Dropped Blockchain. That's the Right Call.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/drex-blockchain/): Brazil's Banco Central spent years building Drex on distributed ledger technology, then abandoned it after the Phase 1 pilot returned a clear verdict.…

- [The Founder's Father Was a Doctor in Colombia Who Died at 58 from a Drug Interaction AI Could Have Caught. a16z Just Led the Series A.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/startups-telepatia-latam-healthtech/): Telepatia raised $33M from a16z, Shyam Sankar, Simón Borrero, and David Vélez — 25+ hospital systems, 14M patients, 60K medical errors prevented in under a year.

- [Brazil Ended Drex. The Privacy Architecture Failed. What Comes Next Is the Real Story.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/reg-drex-fim/): The Banco Central do Brasil shut down the Drex platform after four years and dozens of consortium partners. The privacy architecture proved incompatible…
- [Brazil's AI Law Was Designed to Create Certainty. It May Create the Opposite.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/reg-marco-ia-brasil/): PL 2338/2023 returns to the CÃ¢mara alongside a companion Executive bill. Brazil's 1,400 AI startups draw less than 3% of their capital from abroad â€” a…
- [Brazil's Central Bank Just Banned the eFX Shortcut. Who Built Real Rails and Who Built a Workaround?](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/reg-bcb-efx/): BCB Resolution 561 bans stablecoins and crypto from regulated cross-border settlement, effective October 1. The $6â€”8B monthly eFX market must now move…
- [The Government Pulled the Plug on the World's Most Capable AI Models for 15 Days. Yesterday It Turned One Back On for 100 Companies.](https://insights.kiara.capital/articles/reg-mythos5-sovereign-ai/): On June 12, the US Commerce Department forced Anthropic to shut down Fable 5 and Mythos 5 globally. On June 26, Mythos 5 was partially restored for ~100 US institutions — establishing a new sovereign AI access tier.
